behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical; keys from different parameters never collide
behaviors.justification: This is pure deterministic computation with no external dependencies, I/O, or stateful behavior. The failure boundary is purely algorithmic — if the hash/keying function is wrong, a unit test with varied inputs catches it immediately. No mocks needed; no infrastructure required.
behaviors.test_description: Unit tests with parametrized inputs covering: identical parameter sets (must always produce same key), distinct parameter sets (must never collide), edge cases (empty params, unicode, large values, nested structures, type variations). Verify determinism by running same inputs multiple times and comparing keys.

behaviors.description: TTL for a cache entry is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule (e.g., user profiles = 5 min, product catalog = 1 hour)
behaviors.justification: Pure computation with mocked time and configuration. The failure boundary is the formula itself — wrong multipliers, missing categories, boundary condition bugs. Unit tests with stubbed time and config catch all of these. External config source is possible but unlikely to be the limiting factor.
behaviors.test_description: Unit tests with mocked configuration and time: verify TTL calculation for each category against expected rule, cover boundary cases (TTL at threshold moment, just under, just over), verify missing/unknown categories handled correctly, check time-dependent edge cases (clock boundaries). Parametrize across all configured categories.

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: A unit test mocking memory.usage() would always pass, hiding real failures where actual memory measurement differs from mocked values. REAL-INFRA: The behavior depends on observing actual heap/cache memory consumption. Real failures include: memory measurement inaccuracy, threshold boundary off-by-one, race condition during strategy switch under real load, or eviction failing partway through. These require real memory allocation and genuine threshold crossing.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that actually allocates cache entries (not mocked) until real in-process memory usage measurably exceeds threshold, verifies strategy switches (read strategy state before/after), ensures next evictions use LFU algorithm, test boundary conditions (just under threshold, at threshold, over threshold). If using OS-level memory APIs, may require subprocess/System level.

behaviors.description: A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Mocking PostgreSQL would hide failures in write detection (missed notifications/triggers), row-to-key mapping logic, and invalidation timing. REAL-INFRA: Must interact with actual PostgreSQL database to detect writes. Real failures: database integration missing, invalidation never triggered, wrong rows matched to keys, race condition where cache read happens between write and invalidation, or bulk operations not handled.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test: (1) populate cache with entries for known rows, (2) perform direct PostgreSQL write(s) to those rows, (3) verify cache state before/after, (4) confirm affected entries invalidated, unaffected entries remain, (5) test single-row writes, bulk updates, and concurrent write scenarios. Include negative case: writes to unrelated rows do not invalidate unrelated cache entries.

behaviors.description: Eviction policy removes correct entries — least-recently-used under LRU, least-frequently-used under LFU — when cache is full
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Unit test with mocked access times/frequencies and cache state could pass while real-world timing precision, concurrent access patterns, or clock boundary issues expose bugs in production. REAL-INFRA: Requires actual cache operations, real timing of accesses, and interaction with concurrent modification. Real failures: access tracking wrong, eviction order wrong, off-by-one in LFU/LRU logic, or concurrency race during eviction.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test: (1) fill cache to capacity with multiple entries, (2) perform varied access patterns — for LRU: sequence of timed accesses to different entries, for LFU: varying frequency of accesses to different entries, (3) trigger cache-full condition, (4) verify entries evicted in correct order per algorithm, (5) repeat with different access patterns, concurrent accesses, and mixed LRU/LFU transitions. Validate eviction order matches algorithm guarantees.
